data.narrative:
Second Front Systems is a Series B–stage defense and security company founded by Peter Dixon and Tyler Sweatt that has raised $90M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms across the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $90M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $171M. Peer-median analysis identifies six same-sector, same-stage comparables—Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, Defense Unicorns, and one additional peer—with a median valuation of $1.2B and a range of $1.0B to $1.5B. This method implies $1.2B.

The triangulated analysis produces a wide range: low of $145M, median of $686M, and high of $1.4B. The dispersion reflects a fundamental tension between stage-adjusted capitalization of deployed capital versus peer-set medians, which likely reflect later-stage or higher-revenue peers. Key uncertainties include revenue scale and growth trajectory relative to the comparable set, customer concentration in defense contracts, and the timing of next funding. A reasonable valuation midpoint clusters around $600M–$800M, though the peer median of $1.2B cannot be dismissed without visibility into relative metrics.
